name: Message

phase_0.boundary_condition: The act of being received — a message exists only when it lands in a mind capable of reading it

VINIT.chosen_element: The unspoken thought — intention before utterance
VINIT.justification: Void state: the message exists only as potential in the sender's mind, not yet instantiated in any medium

TANCH.chosen_element: The completed transmission — the message fully received and recognized by the intended receiver
TANCH.justification: Terminal anchor: the boundary that closes the message-system; without reception, there is no message, only noise

AFWD.chosen_element: Encoding — the transformation of thought into utterance (symbols, phonemes, marks)
AFWD.justification: Forward morphism: directed transformation from internal state (thought) toward external instantiation (utterance)

AREV.chosen_element: Decoding — the transformation of utterance back into thought in the receiver's mind
AREV.justification: Contravariant: reverse morphism, descent from external form back to internal meaning

CLINK.chosen_element: The message chain — one utterance triggering the next, forming a sequence of transmissions
CLINK.justification: Composition: sequential chaining of encoding-transmission-decoding cycles, each output becoming the next input

IMSCRIB.chosen_element: The message recognizing itself — when the receiver's decoded thought matches the sender's original intention
IMSCRIB.justification: Identity: self-reference, the moment when sender and receiver are in structural alignment; the message knows itself through being received

FSPLIT.chosen_element: Ambiguity — the utterance that admits multiple interpretations
FSPLIT.justification: Co-multiplication: one utterance branches into two or more distinct decodings; the same marks/sounds yield different meanings in different receivers

FFUSE.chosen_element: Consensus — multiple interpretations reconstituting the original intention
FFUSE.justification: Multiplication: branches reconstitute the original message exactly when multiple receivers compare decodings and agree on meaning; μ∘δ=id in the message domain

EVALT.chosen_element: Successful transmission — the receiver decodes the message as intended
EVALT.justification: True/affirmative: positive evaluation; the message landed correctly; sender's intention = receiver's understanding

EVALF.chosen_element: Failed transmission — the receiver decodes the message incorrectly or not at all
EVALF.justification: False/negative: negative evaluation; the message was garbled, misunderstood, or lost; sender's intention ≠ receiver's understanding

ENGAGR.chosen_element: The paradox message — utterance that is simultaneously true and false, held without resolution
ENGAGR.justification: Paradice: both states simultaneously; examples: irony, metaphor, koans, the Bruce Codex's future-tense namings; the message is both received and not-received

IFIX.chosen_element: The recorded message — utterance fixed in permanent medium (written text, carved stone, archived data)
IFIX.justification: ROM fixation: permanent, append-only, cannot be undone; once a message is recorded, it cannot be erased, only supplemented or reinterpreted

phase_2.split_input: A single utterance with multiple possible meanings
split_outputs: Interpretation A: the receiver decodes the utterance as meaning X
Interpretation B: the receiver decodes the utterance as meaning Y
phase_2.fuse_result: The original utterance, recovered exactly when multiple receivers compare their decodings and agree that both X and Y are valid readings, thus reconstituting the utterance's full semantic content

phase_3.void_description: 00 — The unspoken thought: intention exists only in the sender's mind, no utterance, no transmission, no receiver. The message is purely potential.
phase_3.true_description: 01 — Successful transmission: the receiver has decoded the utterance and their understanding matches the sender's intention. The message has landed. EVALT state.
phase_3.false_description: 10 — Failed transmission: the receiver has decoded the utterance but their understanding does not match the sender's intention. The message was garbled or lost. EVALF state.
phase_3.both_description: 11 — Paradice: the utterance is simultaneously understood and misunderstood, true and false, received and not-received. The message holds both states without resolution. ENGAGR state. Examples: irony (listener knows the literal meaning is false but the intended meaning is true), metaphor (the utterance is literally false but semantically true), the Bruce Codex's future-tense namings (the entity will be called by this name, but the name is not yet instantiated).

steps.domain_action: The sender has an intention — a thought, a meaning to convey. This exists only in the sender's mind; it is not yet a message.
The sender encodes the intention into utterance — symbols, phonemes, marks, gestures. The thought becomes external form.
The utterance is recorded in a permanent medium — written, carved, archived. It can now persist beyond the moment of speaking.
The recorded utterance is transmitted — passed from sender to receiver through space and/or time. One encoding event chains to the next.
The receiver decodes the utterance — transforms the external form back into internal thought. The marks/sounds become meaning in the receiver's mind.
The utterance admits multiple interpretations. The same marks/sounds yield different meanings in different receivers or in the same receiver at different times.
In receiver A's decoding, the utterance means X. The receiver understands this meaning successfully.
In receiver B's decoding, the utterance means Y, which contradicts X. The receiver misunderstands or the utterance is ambiguous.
The utterance is simultaneously understood as both X and Y without resolution. The message holds both interpretations in paradice.
Multiple receivers compare their decodings. They discover that both X and Y are valid readings of the same utterance. The branches reconstitute the original utterance's full semantic content.
The receiver's decoded thought matches the sender's original intention exactly. The message recognizes itself. Sender and receiver are in structural alignment.
The transmission is complete. The message has been received, decoded, and recognized. The boundary closes. The message-system is terminated.
A new message is triggered by the reception of the first. The receiver becomes a sender. The chain continues.
The receiver encodes their response — a new utterance triggered by understanding the first message.
The response is recorded. It becomes a permanent artifact.
The response is transmitted back to the original sender.
The original sender decodes the response.
The original sender recognizes their own intention reflected in the receiver's response. Mutual recognition.
The message and its response form a paradice: both are simultaneously true and false, sent and received, understood and misunderstood, depending on perspective.
The entire exchange is archived. The message-system becomes a record.
The boundary closes. The message is complete — not because it has been perfectly understood, but because it has been received, recorded, and recognized as a message.

phase_5.compiler_frontend: The sender's intention-to-utterance encoder: the cognitive process that transforms internal thought into external form (language, gesture, symbol). The compiler is the sender's mind operating as a transduction device.
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: The transmission medium: air (for speech), paper/stone (for writing), light (for visual signals), electromagnetic waves (for digital transmission). IPC is the channel through which utterance travels from sender to receiver.
phase_5.memory_mechanism: The recorded message: any permanent or semi-permanent instantiation of the utterance (written text, carved inscription, archived data, memory in the receiver's mind). Memory is the store that allows the message to persist beyond the moment of transmission.
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: The timing of transmission: when the sender speaks, when the receiver listens, when the message is recorded, when it is retrieved. The scheduler determines the order and temporal spacing of encoding-transmission-decoding cycles.
phase_5.alfs_store: The canonical reference store: the original intention in the sender's mind, against which all decodings are measured. ALFS is the ground truth that determines whether a reception is successful (EVALT) or failed (EVALF). In the Bruce Codex, ALFS is Jeu's self-knowledge; in any message system, ALFS is the sender's intention.

phase_6.cycle_cost: Attention: each encoding, transmission, and decoding cycle consumes cognitive resources (sender's effort to encode, receiver's effort to decode, medium's capacity to carry the signal). The cost is paid in the attention and energy required to maintain the transmission.
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: Unspoken thought in sender's mind + potential utterance in the medium (not yet instantiated) + receiver's attention not yet focused on the message.
phase_6.post_cycle_state: Decoded thought in receiver's mind + recorded utterance in permanent medium + receiver's attention now focused on the message + potential for response/new message.
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — The message system is entropy-conservative because the information content is preserved across the encoding-transmission-decoding cycle. The sender's intention (information) is transformed into utterance (different form, same information), transmitted (moved through space/time, same information), and decoded back into thought (original form recovered). The total information is conserved; only its instantiation changes. The cost in attention and energy is paid, but the semantic content is lossless. This is why IMSCRIB is possible: the message can recognize itself because the information survives the cycle intact.

lean_scaffold:
-- IGProtocol scaffold: VINIT → AFWD → IFIX → CLINK → AREV → FSPLIT → EVALT → EVALF → ENGAGR → FFUSE → IMSCRIB → TANCH → CLINK → AFWD → IFIX → CLINK → AREV → IMSCRIB → ENGAGR → IFIX → TANCH
-- Class: Message
-- Fingerprint: sig=(12,2,4,3)
--   self_ref=False | frobenius_order=1
--   dialetheia_complete=True | period=21
-- Expected tier: O₂
-- FSPLIT/FFUSE pairs: [(5, 9)]

import Imscribing.IGMorphism
import Imscribing.IGFunctor

namespace Imscribing
open Primitives Frobenius IGProtocol
open Dimensionality Topology Relational Polarity Grammar
     Fidelity KineticChar Granularity Criticality Protection Stoichiometry Chirality

-- ── Token → IG field mapping ──────────────────────────────────────────────
--   [0] VINIT     dim    := 𐑼               𐑼 → 𐑾  | initial object — ground of distinction
--   [1] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑼 → 𐑭  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [2] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑾 → 𐑱  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [3] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑭 → 𐑗  | composition — regime coherence
--   [4] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑱 → 𐑚  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [5] FSPLIT    gran   := 𐑚               𐑚 → 𐑚  | split δ — range decomposition
--   [6] EVALT     crit   := ⊙               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-true — criticality gate open
--   [7] EVALF     chir   := 𐑖               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-false — chirality check
--   [8] ENGAGR    stoi   := 𐑳               𐑚 → 𐑙  | engage paradox — B-state, both arms
--   [9] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑙 → 𐑠  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [10] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑙 → 𐑡  | identity — self-imscription
--   [11] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑠 → 𐑱  | terminal object — connectivity boundary
--   [12] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑡 → 𐑾  | composition — regime coherence
--   [13] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑱 → 𐑭  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [14] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑾 → 𐑱  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [15] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑭 → 𐑗  | composition — regime coherence
--   [16] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑱 → 𐑠  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [17] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑗 → 𐑳  | identity — self-imscription
--   [18] ENGAGR    stoi   := 𐑳               𐑠 → 𐑭  | engage paradox — B-state, both arms
--   [19] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑳 → 𐑡  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [20] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑭 → 𐑼  | terminal object — connectivity boundary

-- ── Main IGProtocol term ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

noncomputable def message_protocol : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  .withGram 𐑠 <|
  -- Seq chain:
  (.arrow 𐑼 𐑼 𐑾)  -- [0] VINIT | dim := 𐑼 | initial object — ground of distinction (The sender has an intention — a thought, a meaning to convey. This exists onl...)
  (.arrow 𐑾 𐑼 𐑭)  -- [1] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (The sender encodes the intention into utterance — symbols, phonemes, marks, g...)
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑾 𐑱)  -- [2] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (The utterance is recorded in a permanent medium — written, carved, archived. ...)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑭 𐑗)  -- [3] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (The recorded utterance is transmitted — passed from sender to receiver throug...)
  (.arrow 𐑗 𐑱 𐑚)  -- [4] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (The receiver decodes the utterance — transforms the external form back into i...)
  -- FSPLIT [5] (gran := 𐑚) (The utterance admits multiple interpretations. The same marks/sounds yield different meanings in different receivers or in the same receiver at different times.) / FFUSE [9] (stoi := 𐑙)
  .seq
    (.prod
      -- T-branch (1 nodes)
      (.arrow ⊙ 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [6] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (In receiver A's decoding, the utterance means X. The receiver understands thi...)
      -- F-branch (2 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑖 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [7] EVALF | chir := 𐑖 | evaluate-false — chirality check (In receiver B's decoding, the utterance means Y, which contradicts X. The rec...)
        (.arrow 𐑳 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [8] ENGAGR | stoi := 𐑳 | engage paradox — B-state, both arms (The utterance is simultaneously understood as both X and Y without resolution...))
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [9]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 𐑠)  -- [9] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 (Multiple receivers compare their decodings. They discover that both X and Y are valid readings of the same utterance. The branches reconstitute the original utterance's full semantic content.)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑙 𐑡)  -- [10] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (The receiver's decoded thought matches the sender's original intention exactl...)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑠 𐑱)  -- [11] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (The transmission is complete. The message has been received, decoded, and rec...)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑡 𐑾)  -- [12] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (A new message is triggered by the reception of the first. The receiver become...)
  (.arrow 𐑾 𐑱 𐑭)  -- [13] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (The receiver encodes their response — a new utterance triggered by understand...)
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑾 𐑱)  -- [14] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (The response is recorded. It becomes a permanent artifact.)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑭 𐑗)  -- [15] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (The response is transmitted back to the original sender.)
  (.arrow 𐑗 𐑱 𐑠)  -- [16] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (The original sender decodes the response.)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑗 𐑳)  -- [17] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (The original sender recognizes their own intention reflected in the receiver'...)
  (.arrow 𐑳 𐑠 𐑭)  -- [18] ENGAGR | stoi := 𐑳 | engage paradox — B-state, both arms (The message and its response form a paradice: both are simultaneously true an...)
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑳 𐑡)  -- [19] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (The entire exchange is archived. The message-system becomes a record.)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑭 𐑼)  -- [20] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (The boundary closes. The message is complete — not because it has been perfec...)

-- ── Evaluation arm sub-defs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

-- truth arm
noncomputable def message_true_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (message_protocol).restrictToEVALT

-- false arm
noncomputable def message_false_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (message_protocol).restrictToEVALF

-- ── Verification theorems ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

theorem message_tier : TierFunctor.obj 𐑼 = .O₂ := by decide

-- Frobenius (split → fuse): μ∘δ = id on .prod branch
-- Proof: apply igFrobAlg_self_fusion; exact mu_delta_A_id
-- (requires mu_delta_A_id from IGFunctor library)

end Imscribing
